example: Nipponanthemum nipponicum, commonly called "Nippon daisy" or "Montauk daisy," is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae.
It is native to coastal regions of Japan but cultivated as an ornamental in other regions.
Ray flowers are white, disc flowers usually yellow but sometimes red or purple.
It is the only species in the genus Nipponanthemum, formerly considered part of Chrysanthemum.
It is now naturalized as an escapee along seashores in New York and New Jersey.
Flower heads are up to 8 cm (3 inches) across and are borne singly.
